title: Egyptian Lamb Kebabs
description: A flavorful and aromatic dish originating from Egypt, featuring tender lamb in a blend of spices and herbs. This recipe is perfect for grilling or broiling.

ingredients.name: Lamb shoulder or leg, cut into 1-inch cubes
ingredients.notes: Fat-trimmed for better flavor and texture

ingredients.name: Roasted garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: Peeled and crushed for maximum flavor

ingredients.name: Paprika
ingredients.notes: For a smoky, slightly sweet flavor

ingredients.name: Coriander powder
ingredients.notes: For a citrusy, slightly sweet flavor

instructions: In a large bowl, combine lamb cubes, roasted garlic, parsley, olive oil, salt, black pepper, cumin powder, paprika, and coriander powder.
Mix well to coat lamb cubes evenly, then cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat grill or broiler to medium-high heat.
Thread lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Grill or broil for 8-10 minutes per side, or until lamb reaches an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C).
Let kebabs rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve hot with your favorite sides, such as couscous, rice, or roasted vegetables.
